subject: Re: shamatha project update and Cliff in NYC Oct 19
body:
please call first week of oct.

--- Clifford Saron <csaron@comcast.net> wrote:

> Hi Jeffrey - I know it's been a long time since
> you've heard from me. We've
> had a very busy summer on our project and we are
> just finishing up intaking
> 134 serious applicants to the project. After careful
> evaluation of most of
> the applicants it looks like we will have over 60
> highly motivated and
> qualified practitioners to test, so the project has
> a new level of
> feasibility.  We've gained cooperation from the
> administration of the
> Shambhala Mountain Center to additionally test staff
> members at the center
> to begin to address the "environmental control"
> questions that we talked
> about in our conversation. We are also deeply
> interested in testing the
> participants in the second retreat - these are the
> people who are the
> controls for the first retreat, so we will have
> extensive baseline
> information on them and they can serve as their own
> controls. This aspect of
> the project will effectively double the number of
> retreat participants and
> increase the statistical power of our analyses. I'm
> presently structuring
> the budget to accomplish these goals and will submit
> to you a completed
> proposal toward the end of the month. 
> 
>  
> 
> I don't know if you've had a chance to look at the
> project description I
> sent a while back. I will be in New York on Oct 19th
> with time to meet with
> you if that works with your schedule. I am also
> happy to talk with you at
> any time. Hope you had a great summer,
> 
>  
> 
> My best,
> 
> Cliff
> 
>  
> 
> Clifford Saron, Ph.D.
> 
> Assistant Research Scientist
> 
> UC Davis Center for Mind and Brain
> 
> 267 Cousteau Place
> 
> Davis, CA 95616
> 
> 415-307-9757 mobile
> 
> cdsaron@ucdavis.edu
> 
> http://mindbrain.ucdavis.edu
> 
>  
> 
>  
> 
>

body_html:
please call first week of oct.

--- Clifford Saron csaron@comcast.net wrote:

Hi Jeffrey - I know it's been a long time since
you've heard from me. We've
had a very busy summer on our project and we are
just finishing up intaking
134 serious applicants to the project. After careful
evaluation of most of
the applicants it looks like we will have over 60
highly motivated and
qualified practitioners to test, so the project has
a new level of
feasibility. We've gained cooperation from the
administration of the
Shambhala Mountain Center to additionally test staff
members at the center
to begin to address the "environmental control"
questions that we talked
about in our conversation. We are also deeply
interested in testing the
participants in the second retreat - these are the
people who are the
controls for the first retreat, so we will have
extensive baseline
information on them and they can serve as their own
controls. This aspect of
the project will effectively double the number of
retreat participants and
increase the statistical power of our analyses. I'm
presently structuring
the budget to accomplish these goals and will submit
to you a completed
proposal toward the end of the month.

I don't know if you've had a chance to look at the
project description I
sent a while back. I will be in New York on Oct 19th
with time to meet with
you if that works with your schedule. I am also
happy to talk with you at
any time. Hope you had a great summer,

My best,

Cliff

Clifford Saron, Ph.D.

Assistant Research Scientist

UC Davis Center for Mind and Brain

267 Cousteau Place

Davis, CA 95616

415-307-9757 mobile

cdsaron@ucdavis.edu

http://mindbrain.ucdavis.edu
